entries.problem: api/discord.js appelle .json() sur une réponse Yahoo Finance qui peut être text/event-stream (SSE), causant une erreur silencieuse ou un crash
entries.commit: fix: cancel SSE response body in discord.js to prevent resource leak on unexpected content-type
entries.learned: Quand on détecte un content-type inattendu (SSE/stream), il faut explicitement annuler le body avec r.body?.cancel() pour éviter que la connexion reste ouverte et consomme des ressources dans la serverless function.

entries.note: Patterns detected before Claude analysis — guiding fix priority

entries.problem: fetch() calls in api/brain.js and api/trading-expert.js without AbortSignal.timeout() can hang indefinitely on internal/external HTTP calls
entries.commit: fix: add AbortSignal.timeout(8000) to discord() helper in brain.js to prevent indefinite hang
entries.learned: Fire-and-forget Discord notification helpers are easy to overlook for timeout guards since errors are swallowed by .catch(() => {}), but without a timeout they can silently block the entire serverless function budget.

entries.problem: fetch() call in api/morning.js without AbortSignal.timeout() on discordPost and generateAIBriefing Anthropic call can hang indefinitely
entries.commit: fix: add AbortSignal.timeout and error handling to discordPost in morning.js
entries.learned: Helper functions that wrap fetch() are easy to overlook for timeout coverage since the pattern is applied at call sites but forgotten in shared utilities.

entries.note: Patterns detected before Claude analysis — guiding fix priority

entries.problem: fetch() calls in api/andy.js without AbortSignal.timeout() on the Anthropic API call (missing in runServerTool's internal calls) — but more critically, the Andy.jsx frontend fetches GitHub API and internal /api/memory without any timeout or error handling on .json() parsing
entries.commit: fix: guard fetch_price tool against SSE response and missing previousClose in api/andy.js
entries.learned: The fetch_price tool in api/andy.js was calling .json() on Yahoo Finance without checking content-type (SSE risk) and was dividing by meta.previousClose which can be undefined/null causing NaN in change/pct calculations.

entries.reason: fetch_price and fetch_crypto_price in api/andy.js already use AbortSignal.timeout(). The Anthropic agentic loop call is inside the exported handler which is not shown in the truncated source. The [MEDIUM] flag for api/andy.js likely refers to the top-level Anthropic messages call in the handler body (not shown). Without seeing that code, a safe targeted fix cannot be produced without risk of introducing errors. The discord.js [CRITICAL] SSE issue was already fixed in a previous cycle. All visible fetch() calls in the provided snippets already have AbortSignal.timeout() applied.

entries.note: Patterns detected before Claude analysis — guiding fix priority

entries.problem: src/pages/Andy.jsx: fetch() calls in ActivityFeed use .then(r => r.json()) without checking r.ok or content-type, and without any AbortSignal.timeout() — a non-JSON or error response (e.g. 401, 500, SSE) will silently crash the Promise.allSettled chain or produce garbage state
entries.commit: fix: add AbortSignal.timeout, ok-check and safe JSON parsing to ActivityFeed fetch calls in Andy.jsx
entries.learned: Frontend fetch chains that call .then(r => r.json()) directly before Promise.allSettled can cause loading states to hang forever when an API returns a non-JSON error response, because the rejection escapes the allSettled boundary.
